title: Bunny Chow
blurb: Durban's iconic street food — a hollowed-out loaf of white bread filled to the brim with aromatic Durban curry.
ings: 400 ×lamb knuckles or chicken thighs, bone-in, cut into pieces

title: Home Chicken Curry
blurb: The everyday SA family chicken curry — gentle aromatic warmth, tender chicken, a rich tomato-onion gravy.

title: Umngqusho (Samp & Beans)
blurb: Nelson Mandela's favourite dish — samp and sugar beans slow-cooked into a creamy, comforting porridge.

title: Vetkoek with Curried Mince
blurb: South African comfort — pillowy golden fried dough split open and filled with sweet, spiced curried mince.

title: Beef Teriyaki
blurb: Japanese home classic - beef grilled with sweet-savoury soy glaze.

title: Cacio e Pepe
blurb: Rome's minimalist masterpiece: spaghetti tossed with Pecorino Romano and black pepper into a creamy emulsion.
ings: 1 tspCoarsely ground black pepper, plus more for serving
55 gPecorino Romano, very finely grated (about 250 ml), plus more for serving

title: Chicken Satay
blurb: Tender marinated chicken skewers grilled over charcoal, served with rich peanut sauce — Indonesia's street food icon.
ings: 800 ×boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into 2.5cm pieces
3lemongrass stalks, white part only, finely chopped

title: Chicken Stir-Fry
blurb: Juicy chicken wok-fried with ginger-garlic, crisp veg and a glossy soy glaze — faster than takeout.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
blurb: Tender grilled chicken in a creamy tomato sauce — Britain's adopted national dish with Indian soul.
ings: 800 ×boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into 3cm pieces

title: Crêpes
blurb: France's whisper-thin pancakes — lacy, golden and endlessly foldable, sweet or savoury.
ings: 25 gbutter, melted, plus extra for frying

title: Croissants
blurb: The French bakery classic - flaky, buttery crescents of laminated dough.

title: Egg Fried Rice
blurb: Day-old rice wok-fried with egg, spring onion and soy — faster, better and cheaper than takeout.

title: Falafel Plate
blurb: The Middle Eastern staple — crispy chickpea fritters with tahini sauce, hummus, fresh herbs.

title: Flapjacks (American Pancakes)
blurb: American breakfast classic - fluffy buttermilk pancakes with maple syrup.

title: Gemista (Stuffed Peppers & Aubergine)
blurb: Greek summer classic - vegetables stuffed with rice, herbs, and tomatoes, baked until tender.

title: Macaroni Cheese
blurb: Tender macaroni in a creamy cheese sauce, topped with buttery crumbs — America's ultimate comfort food.

title: Penne Arrabbiata
blurb: Angry Roman tomato sauce — garlic, chilli and tomato, clingy with ridged penne.
ings: 1 tspdried chilli flakes (or 1 fresh red chilli, finely chopped)

title: Prego Roll
blurb: Seared steak in a soft roll with garlic-peri-peri butter — the Portuguese-SA steak sandwich.
ings: 1 tbspperi-peri sauce (or chilli flakes to taste)

title: Quesadillas
blurb: Mexican street classic - flour tortillas filled with cheese and folded until melty.

title: Quiche Lorraine
blurb: France's brunch classic — buttery pastry with rich bacon-Gruyère custard.

title: Shakshuka with Crusty Bread
blurb: Eggs gently poached in a spiced tomato sauce — a North African breakfast now loved across the Middle East.
method: Braise · egg poach in tomato sauce

title: Smash Burgers with Special Sauce
blurb: The burger technique that changed everything: thin patties smashed onto a screaming-hot griddle for maximum crispy crust.
ings: 220 ×Freshly beef mince chuck (80/20 fat ratio), divided into 2 × 55g balls

title: Spaghetti Bolognese
blurb: The SA 'spag bol' — a slow, deep beef ragù with soffrito, tomato and a finish of milk, tossed through spaghetti.

title: Spaghetti Carbonara
blurb: Four ingredients, no cream — the Roman classic where technique is everything and the sauce is silk.
ings: 100 gguanciale, diced — pancetta or streaky bacon work

title: Spanakopita
blurb: Greece's spinach pie — crisp phyllo layers with spinach, feta, and herbs.

title: Thai Green Curry
blurb: The most popular curry in Thailand: coconut milk spiked with green chillies, aromatic with basil and lime leaves.
ings: 225 gThai aubergine, quartered (or 1 × 540g can bamboo shoots)

title: French Onion Soup, Gruyère Crouton
blurb: Caramelised onions simmered in beef broth, topped with toasted baguette and melted Gruyère — French comfort at its finest.

title: Churros & Chocolate
blurb: Crisp ridged batons, rolled in sugar, with a cup of dark chocolate.

title: Bobotie
blurb: South Africa's national dish — sweet, spiced Cape Malay mince baked under a savoury egg-custard lid, perfumed with bay and served with yellow rice.

title: Breyani
blurb: Durban's celebratory feast — layered rice and meat in aromatic spices, the crown of Durban Indian cuisine.
ings: 2 tbspbiryani masala or mild curry powder

title: Cape Malay Chicken Curry
blurb: The fragrant, gently sweet Western Cape curry — chicken and potato in a roasted-wet-masala gravy of coriander, cumin, fennel and turmeric, lifted with apricot and finished with coriander.
ings: 500 gmixed chicken pieces, skinless (thighs and drumsticks)

title: Denningvleis
blurb: Cape Malay's sweet-sour masterpiece — lamb slow-cooked with cinnamon, tamarind and vinegar in a complex melody of flavours.

title: Doro Wat (Ethiopian Chicken)
blurb: Ethiopia's celebratory stew — fiery berbere-spiced chicken with hard-boiled eggs, served with injera.

title: Durban Mutton Curry
blurb: Durban's soul curry — tender mutton on the bone in a rich, fiery gravy redolent with curry leaves and aromatic spices.
ings: 800 ×mutton on the bone (neck, rib or shoulder), cut into pieces

title: Hertzoggies
blurb: Afrikaans tartlets — buttery pastry filled with apricot jam and topped with fluffy coconut meringue.

title: Melktert
blurb: South Africa's milk tart — a silken cinnamon custard in a buttery biscuit crust, finished with a snowfall of cinnamon.

title: Peppermint Crisp Tart
blurb: The ultimate South African fridge tart — layers of coconut biscuits, caramel condensed milk, whipped cream and crushed Peppermint Crisp.

title: Pickled Fish
blurb: Cape Malay's Easter classic — fried white fish steeped in a sweet-sour curry-spiced vinegar with onions.

title: Shisa Nyama
blurb: South Africa's township barbecue tradition — buy raw meat from the butchery, grill it over hot coals, and eat with music and community.
ings: 400 ×rump or sirloin steak, cut into 3cm cubes
20 mlperi-peri sauce or mild chilli sauce

title: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)
blurb: The creamy tomato curry South Africa cooks most at home — tandoori-style chicken in a silky makhani sauce of tomato, butter, cream and warm spices, finished with fenugreek.
ings: 350 gboneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 3 cm pieces
150 gripe tomatoes, chopped (or 125 ml tomato passata)
20 graw cashews, soaked in hot water

title: Carnitas Tacos Michoacán
blurb: The authentic Michoacán way: pork slow-cooked in lard until fork-tender, then crisped and tucked into warm corn tortillas.
ings: 2 ×Mixed pork cuts (see brief): shoulder, skin, belly, ribs, hocks
120 mlSalsa verde or salsa de arbol

title: Chicken Biryani
blurb: Indian celebration rice - layered chicken and rice with saffron, spices, and fried onions.

title: Crème Brûlée
blurb: French restaurant classic — silky vanilla custard with caramelised sugar crust.

title: Crispy-Skin Salmon, Brown Butter & Capers
blurb: Glass-crisp skin, blushing centre, nutty brown butter — the fast date dish that looks like far more work.
ings: 2 ×salmon fillets, skin on, scaled & pin-boned, ~180 g

title: Eggs Benedict, Hollandaise
blurb: Brunch classic - poached eggs on ham-topped English muffins with hollandaise.

title: Fettuccine Alfredo
blurb: Silky parmesan-butter emulsion — luxurious creaminess with no cream, the Roman original.

title: Grilled Calamari
blurb: Calamari scored and seared fast, dressed with garlic-lemon butter — simple, elegant seafood.
ings: 400 gcalamari tubes, cleaned and patted dry

title: Lamb Tagine, Apricot & Almond
blurb: Morocco's fragrant stew — tender lamb with dried apricots, almonds, and aromatic spices.

title: Lasagne
blurb: Bologna's pride — slow-cooked meat ragù, creamy béchamel, fresh pasta layered and baked.

title: Molten Chocolate Fondant
blurb: The ultimate dinner party dessert: individual chocolate cakes with molten centres, ready in 12 minutes from the oven.

title: Moules Marinières
blurb: The classic sailor-style mussels: steamed open in white wine with garlic, shallots, and butter — the taste of Brittany and Belgium.

title: Moussaka (Greek Aubergine and Beef Casserole)
blurb: Iconic Greek casserole with layers of aubergine, rich beef mince, and creamy béchamel topping.

title: Peri-Peri Chicken Livers
blurb: Flash-fried chicken livers in a garlic-peri-peri cream sauce — rich, spicy, on the table in minutes.
ings: 2 tbspperi-peri sauce (or chilli flakes to taste)

title: Pizza Margherita
blurb: Neapolitan-style pizza at home — vibrant tomato, melting mozzarella, fresh basil, on a blistered crust.
ings: 300 gpizza dough (store-bought is fine), at room temperature
150 gSan Marzano or good tinned plum tomatoes, crushed by hand

title: Potato Gnocchi
blurb: Store-bought gnocchi pan-seared crisp, then glossed in nutty brown butter and crispy sage.

title: Risotto Milanese with Saffron
blurb: The golden rice of Milan, transformed by saffron into one of Italy's most luxurious dishes.

title: Roast Chicken, Lemon, Garlic & Herbs
blurb: The Sunday centrepiece — golden, juicy, perfumed with herbs. The dish that says you can host.

title: Rogan Josh
blurb: Aromatic Kashmiri lamb curry with warming spices — the royal dish of the Wazwan feast.
ings: 1 kglamb shoulder, bone-in, cut into 4cm pieces
1saffron threads, bloomed in 2 tbsp warm water

title: Sticky Toffee Pudding
blurb: Britain's favourite pudding — a date-sweetened sponge drenched in warm toffee sauce, served with cream or ice cream.

title: Tiramisu
blurb: Italy's coffee classic — espresso-dipped ladyfingers with mascarpone cream, dusted with cocoa.

title: Braai Lamb Chops, Chimichurri
blurb: Lamb chops grilled over coals, served with vibrant Argentine chimichurri sauce.

title: Coq au Vin
blurb: Chicken braised in red Burgundy wine with bacon, mushrooms, and pearl onions — France's rustic masterpiece.
ings: 750 mldry red Burgundy or Pinot Noir

title: Duck à l'Orange
blurb: Roast duck with a classic orange sauce — sweet, sharp, and savoury, the dish that defined French haute cuisine.
ings: 1 ×whole duck (Pekin), ~2.5 kg, trimmed of excess fat
2 tbsporange zest, plus strips for garnish
1 tbspcornflour, dissolved in 2 tbsp cold water

title: Mozambican Peri-Peri Chicken
blurb: Mozambique's fiery gift — chicken marinated in lemon, garlic and African bird's eye chilli, grilled to charred, spicy perfection.
ings: 30 ×African bird's eye chillis (fresh or dried), stemmed

title: Pan-Seared Steak, Butter & Thyme
blurb: A great steak is less recipe than ritual — salt early, hot pan, baste in foaming butter and thyme.
ings: 2 ×ribeye or sirloin steaks, ~250 g each, 3–4 cm thick

title: Red-Wine Braised Lamb Shank
blurb: Lamb shanks slowly braised in red wine until falling-off-the-bone tender — the ultimate make-ahead dinner party dish.

title: Red-Wine Braised Short Rib
blurb: Modern classic - beef short ribs slowly braised in red wine until falling apart.

title: Sosaties
blurb: Cape Malay skewers — lamb and dried apricots marinated in a tangy curry-spiced sauce, grilled to sticky perfection.

title: Steak Frites, Café de Paris Butter
blurb: Pan-seared steak served with crispy frites and Café de Paris butter — the bistro classic that defined French brasserie dining.

title: Koeksisters
blurb: South Africa's sticky-sweet plaits — crisp fried dough plunged into ice-cold syrup so the sugar shatters into crystals.
ings: 12 cm piece fresh ginger, peeled and bruised

title: Malva Pudding
blurb: South Africa's Sunday favourite — caramelised apricot sponge with hot cream-butter sauce.

title: Potjiekos
blurb: South Africa's fireside feast — meat and vegetables layered, never stirred, and simmered for hours in a three-legged cast-iron potjie.

title: Beef Ribs with Sticky Glaze
blurb: Slow-braised beef ribs lacquered in a sweet-savoury glaze — falling-off-the-bone, restaurant-quality.

title: Espetada
blurb: Beef cubes marinated in garlic, bay and vinegar, grilled on skewers — a Madeiran classic at the SA braai.
ings: 500 grump or sirloin, cut into 3–4 cm cubes

title: Roast Leg of Lamb, Rosemary & Garlic
blurb: Easter Sunday classic - leg of lamb roasted with rosemary, garlic, and lemon.

title: Boerewors
blurb: South Africa's national sausage — coarse beef sausage spiced with toasted coriander, grilled to smoky perfection over the coals.
ings: 200 ×pork fat or beef fat, cubed and frozen

title: Chakalaka
blurb: South Africa's fiery relish — onions, peppers, carrots and baked beans simmered with curry spice, the heartbeat of any braai plate.

title: Focaccia
blurb: Liguria's olive-oil flatbread — a dimpled, pillowy crumb with a crackling golden bottom, scattered with sea salt and rosemary.

title: Mealie Bread
blurb: Sweet corn bread — moist, golden and flecked with corn kernels, a beloved braai side and tea-time treat.

title: Naan Bread
blurb: Indian flatbread classic - soft, pillowy naan baked in a hot tandoor or oven.

title: Pap (Mealie Meal)
blurb: South Africa's daily bread — maize meal porridge, from crumbly phutu to smooth slap pap, the base of every meal.

title: Potbrood
blurb: South Africa's fireside bread — a soft, golden loaf baked in a lidded cast-iron pot for a crackling crust and pillowy crumb.
method: Fire or oven · bake in a potjie
ings: 30 gbutter, melted, plus extra for greasing

title: Roosterkoek
blurb: Braai bread at its best — yeasted dough balls cooked over hot coals, crisp outside and pillowy soft within.
